sources.title: Event Sourcing with Apache Kafka at Scale
AxonIQ: Event Sourcing Patterns and Anti-Patterns

result.summary: Event sourcing at scale requires careful attention to snapshotting strategies, event schema evolution, and projection rebuilding capabilities, with production systems typically handling 50K-500K events/second using partitioned Kafka topics or purpose-built event stores.
key_findings: Snapshotting every N events (typically 100-500) is essential for read performance; without it, aggregate reconstruction times grow linearly and become untenable beyond 10K events per aggregate
Schema evolution is the most common pain point: Avro with a schema registry or Protobuf with backward-compatible changes are preferred over JSON for production event stores
CQRS is nearly always paired with event sourcing at scale, with separate read models materialized via projections that can be rebuilt independently from the event log
